cards.meaning: on the contrary; instead (the result is the opposite of what you'd expect)
cards.examplePinyin: bù dàn méi zhǎng, fǎn'ér diē le
cards.exampleMeaning: Not only didn't it rise — on the contrary, it fell.
cards.note: HSK-4 advanced connective; register leans 书面语/analytical — a finance-desk word. Signature collocation: 不但没…反而… ('not only didn't… but on the contrary…'). Near-synonym contrast vs 却 (què): 却 is a mild 'however,' a gentle pivot; 反而 is stronger and pointed — an outcome that actively defies the expectation you just set up.
cards.tiesTo: the market rotation — yields fell instead of rising; homebuilders ripped while megacap tech sold off

cards.example: 价格稳定是央行的首要目标。
cards.examplePinyin: jiàgé wěndìng shì yāngháng de shǒuyào mùbiāo
cards.exampleMeaning: Price stability is the central bank's primary objective.
cards.note: HSK-4 abstract collocation, formal/policy register. Portable: 价格稳定 (price stability), plus grid / currency / financial stability. Near-synonym contrast vs 平稳 (píngwěn): 稳定 is a STATE — the thing is stable, full stop; 平稳 is a smooth PROCESS — steady, no jolts (e.g. 平稳着陆, a soft landing). State versus journey.
cards.tiesTo: BSP / ECB / Fed price-stability mandates and grid stability in the energy block

cards.meaning: the southwest monsoon — the hot, wet, stormy season-wind
cards.example: Pinalakas ng habagat ang ulan sa kanlurang bahagi ng bansa.
cards.exampleMeaning: The habagat intensified the rain over the western part of the country.
cards.note: Native-speaker pairing: habagat (southwest monsoon — humid, rainy, the flooded-street months) versus amihan (the northeast monsoon — the cool, dry 'ber-months' air everyone welcomes). Same idea, a prevailing seasonal wind, but opposite feeling: one you brace for, one you welcome.
cards.tiesTo: Typhoon Francisco enhancing the southwest monsoon over Luzon

cards.meaning: relief, ease, a sense of well-being / breathing room
cards.example: Bumababa ang presyo ng langis, kaya may kaunting ginhawa ang mga pamilya.
cards.exampleMeaning: Oil prices are falling, so families get a little relief.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance vs luwag (both render as 'loosening'): luwag is the mechanical slack — looser policy, a belt with another notch — the CAUSE; ginhawa is the FELT result, the comfort and breathing room you actually experience. Cheaper oil gives the peso some luwag; what households feel is ginhawa. The rich form, kaginhawaan, means welfare/prosperity outright.
cards.tiesTo: the oil reversal easing the Philippines' import bill, inflation path, and peso
